title: centaurea aksamica
intro: Centaurea aksamica, also known as Aksamica knapweed, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family Asteraceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in the treatment of various skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. Centaurea aksamica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
